title: Associate Director, Technical Operations
description:
Job Responsibilities:

  • Provide necessary resources to ensure personnel obtain qualifications, training, and assessments.
  • Ensure deviations are investigated promptly and corrective and preventive actions are implemented.
  • Ensure daily operations comply with GMP/EHS standards and meet customer expectations.
  • Define staffing criteria for key positions and participate in final interviews and hiring decisions for critical roles (especially direct reports and key personnel).
  • Decide on priorities, allocating limited budgets, manpower, and time to specific projects or technical directions.
  • Design or adjust the team structure, division of labor, and collaboration mechanisms according to departmental development.
  • Intervene, assess, and manage significant incidents or severe interpersonal conflicts, taking ultimate responsibility.
  • Conduct performance evaluations, make salary adjustment recommendations, and personnel decisions for direct reports.
  • Establish and monitor non-negotiable standards concerning quality, safety, compliance, and financial matters.
  • Team Building:
  • Set clear goals for the team, ensuring each member understands the significance of their work and fosters synergy.
  • Build a highly efficient team, bringing out the initiative, enthusiasm, and creativity of each member.
  • Facilitate effective communication and cooperation between departments, encouraging cross-functional collaboration.
  • Train and develop subordinate employees, enhancing their work skills and efficiency.
  • Pay attention to project progress, promptly identify difficulties, and provide critical support.
  • Secure and reasonably allocate the necessary manpower, budget, tools, and information to achieve team goals.

Competencies:

  • Over 15 years of R&D and production experience, with more than 6 years in CDMO project management.
  • Strong awareness of quality and compliance, familiar with operational documentation systems, and ensure project execution meets regulatory requirements; well-versed in production operations and understanding of GMP production systems and documentation requirements.
  • Proficient in reading and writing in both English and Chinese, capable of fluent technical communication with both the local Singapore team and the technical team in China.
  • Excellent communication and coordination skills, capable of effectively integrating internal and external resources to advance projects.
  • Strong leadership and subordinates development skills, outstanding execution and problem-solving abilities, efficiently driving projects and coordinating cross-departmental resources.
  • Strong problem-solving skills, capable of correctly guiding and supervising subordinates' work.
  • Innovative abilities, proficient in production system operations and training, and familiar with equipment principles.
  • Training capabilities, actively summarizing and reflecting, forming standardized documents, training employees, and tracking execution results.
  • Strong adaptability, quickly adjusting to new environments, and respecting cultural diversity.
  • Strong learning ability, capable of quickly understanding and applying new cultures, equipment, technologies, and electronic systems.
